channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Leaked renders of the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7 FE may have revealed the affordable foldable's design
item.description: This could be our first look at the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7 FE, which is due to launch in four months' time.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to free up space on your Mac
item.description: If you need to free up space on your Mac, consider deleting duplicate photos in your image library. Apple makes it easy to do, and the CyberGuy explains the process.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk tries to rehab DOGE after backlash, mistakes
item.description: Elon Musk is pushing to improve the image of his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), suggesting the Tesla CEO is aware of the boogeyman persona he has created for himself and his cost-cutting push since joining President Trump’s team.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: A Nvidia chip shortage is coming, warns major Chinese server maker
item.description: One of China’s largest server makers, H3C, has flagged potential shortages of Nvidia’s H20 chip, the most advanced AI processor legally available domestically under U.S. export controls, in a client notice seen by Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Apple, Meta face modest fines as EU seeks to avoid US spat
item.description: Apple Inc. and Meta Platforms Inc. are set to be hit by fines under the European Union's Big Tech competition rulebook toward the end of next week, with the iPhone maker facing the added threat of recurring penalties under the bloc's Digital Markets Act.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: A future Apple Watch could debut a glass frame with touch controls
item.description: A new leak and patent indicate that Apple is working on a future Apple Watch with a touchscreen glass frame.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: Convicted of bilking investors, Nikola founder and Trump donor gets a presidential pardon
item.description: The founder of an electric car start-up sentenced to prison for exaggerating the potential of his technology has been pardoned by President Donald Trump, potentially wiping out hundreds of millions of dollars in restitution that prosecutors were seeking for bamboozled investors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bay Area protests planned for this weekend targeting Tesla, Musk and Trump
item.description: The Tesla Takedown leaders insist that the foundation of their protest movement is that it be a peaceful one.
item.dc:creator: Rick Hurd, The Mercury News

item.title: What is 'classified' information? What are 'secure' communications? Here's a primer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's use of a popular messaging app to discuss sensitive military plans — with a journalist on the text chain — is raising questions about security and the importance of safeguarding the nation's secrets.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An AI pioneer is launching a blockchain-powered marketplace for open-source models and applications
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien And Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Los Angeles sheriff's department to retest 4,000 DNA samples after using faulty kits for 8 months
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department said this week it will retest 4,000 DNA samples and open an internal investigation after learning that it used potentially flawed test kits for eight months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Preserve loved ones' voicemails on iPhone
item.description: Saving the voices of loved ones can be a comforting way to keep alive memories. Kurt "the CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how to preserve voicemail messages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Red Hat’s AI portfolio evolves to address enterprise deployment challenges
item.description: Red Hat announced updates to Red Hat OpenShift AI and Red Hat Enterprise Linux AI (RHEL AI), with a goal of addressing the high costs and technical complexity of scaling AI beyond pilot projects into full deployment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cyprus to fund desalination plants at hotels to reduce impact of water shortages on tourism
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — Cyprus said Wednesday it plans to subsidize construction of private desalination plants at hotels to ensure the tourism-reliant island nation has enough fresh water too see it through busy summer seasons when the demands of millions of visitors put a severe strain on dwindling
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tiny electric vehicle takes half a parking space
item.description: Toyota's compact electric FT-Me is designed to tackle challenges of city living while offering an accessible and eco-friendly transportation solution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The secret to using generative AI effectively
item.description: Do you think generative AI (genAI) sucks? I did. The hype around everything genAI has been over the top and ridiculous for a while now. Especially at the start, most of the tools were flashy, but quickly fell apart if you tried to use them for any serious work purposes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Gee whiz, Gboard! An invaluable new Android typing upgrade
item.description: Ahh — don’t you just love the feeling of a fresh ‘n’ zesty virtual upgrade? Here in the land o’ Android, that doesn’t mean only major new Android versions (and, alas, the woeful waiting that often accompanies ’em).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What you need to know about Go, Rust, and Zig
item.description: Every language has a life cycle. Sometimes it starts with a relatively narrow use case and escapes its container; sometimes it’s intended as a general-purpose language but finds a powerful niche instead.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Open-source Styrolite project aims to simplify container runtime security
item.description: Today Edera launched a new open-source project called Styrolite to bring tighter controls to the interactions between containers and Linux kernel namespaces, at a layer below where Open Container Initiative (OCI) runtimes like containerd operate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Intro to Alpine.js: A JavaScript framework for minimalists
item.description: I recently backpacked through Big Sur, and after a few days, the inevitable happened: I looked at everything I carried and demanded it justify its presence in my backpack. Making tech choices during the software development process is similar.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Vibe coding is groovy
item.description: Vibe coding is most definitely having a moment. Don’t feel bad if you haven’t heard of it—the Wikipedia page for it just went up on March 15 of this year.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoD shepherds CMMC through Trump deregulatory initiative
item.description: The Trump administration has a '10 out, one in' rule for new regulations, but DoD officials are confident they can finalize the CMMC requirements soon. The post DoD shepherds CMMC through Trump deregulatory initiative first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Striking the balance between information sharing and security
item.description: That balancing act requires a complex response, especially when designing networks and systems for collaboration. The post Striking the balance between information sharing and security first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Napster sold to tech commerce company for $207 million
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A brand that was notoriously connected to music piracy before reemerging as a subscription music service has been sold to Infinite Reality for $207 million.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two new AI tools help illuminate GSA’s future state
item.description: GSA's acting Administrator Stephen Ehikian said that the agency is breaking down the data silos to allow systems to better communicate with one another. The post Two new AI tools help illuminate GSA’s future state first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: The nation’s statisticians worry about the state of federal data gathering
item.description: One of the government's activities is the gathering of reliable statistics to guide policy and the quality of national statistics has become a concern. The post The nation’s statisticians worry about the state of federal data gathering first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Waymo plans to bring its driverless taxis to Washington in 2026
item.description: Waymo on Tuesday added Washington to its pioneering robotaxi service's steadily expanding list of U.S. markets, although passengers will have to wait until next year until they can take a driverless ride around the nation's capital city.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fauna to shut down FaunaDB service in May
item.description: Fauna, the provider of the NoSQL database FaunaDB, has said that it will shut down the service by the end of May due to the unavailability of capital required to support the database service and market it.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Scary AI-powered swarm robots team up to build cars faster than ever
item.description: UBTech and Zeekr unite with AI robot swarms to make car manufacturing faster and smarter. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how the process works.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 20 powerful women shaping the networking industry
item.description: Women are severely underrepresented in top leadership roles across the business world. Only 10.4% of the Fortune 500 companies have women CEOs. In an AP survey of S&P 500 companies, only 25 of 341 CEOs were women. That disparity extends into the technology sector.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google's Acquisition of Wiz: A Step Forward in Multicloud Security
item.description: Google's acquisition of Wiz positions the tech giant as a leader in tackling multicloud challenges, potentially outperforming competitors like Microsoft Azure and AWS.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: In 2025, you can’t have an effective democracy without data literacy
item.description: Data literacy should be a part of the 21st-century civics curriculum. The post In 2025, you can’t have an effective democracy without data literacy first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: A European startup scrubs its attempt to launch an orbital rocket on its first test flight
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — A private European aerospace company scrubbed its attempt on Monday to launch the first test flight of its orbital launch vehicle from Norway.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
